title: Salty Springy Venezuelan Pecorino-Romano Grated En-Croute
description: A post-workout dish featuring the salty, springy flavors of Venezuelan cuisine, made with pecorino-romano and en-croute ingredients. This recipe is sugar-free-friendly.
instructions: Thaw the pastry dough according to package instructions.
In a bowl, mix together pecorino-romano cheese, lemon juice, and garlic powder (if using).
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out a rectangle of dough slightly larger than the copper mug.
Place the dough rectangle over the mug, pressing edges to seal.
Fill the dough-lined mug with the pecorino-romano mixture, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Top with sliced cucumber and sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste.
Fold the dough edges over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Bake for 15 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Let cool for 2 minutes, then serve warm, garnished with chopped cilantro.
